metadata.title: Iran-Israel Conflict Damage Tracker
metadata.description: Comprehensive mapping of all confirmed, reported, and rumored damage from the Iran-Israel conflict across all countries, covering civilian, infrastructure, military, and nuclear facility damage.

damageLocations.name: IRGC Aerospace Force HQ
damageLocations.description: Underground command bunkers destroyed by GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators delivered by B-2 Spirit bombers. At least 40 senior IRGC officers killed including two brigadier generals.

damageLocations.name: Supreme Leader's Complex (Niavaran)
damageLocations.description: Tomahawk cruise missile strikes on Khamenei's residential and administrative complex. Significant structural damage confirmed. Khamenei reportedly relocated hours prior.

damageLocations.name: Ministry of Defense Compound
damageLocations.description: JASSM-ER cruise missiles collapsed multiple buildings and destroyed the central communications hub at the Iranian Ministry of Defense.

damageLocations.name: Imam Khomeini Airport Military Depot
damageLocations.description: Satellite imagery suggests military logistics depot adjacent to Imam Khomeini International Airport was struck. Secondary explosions visible for hours suggest ammunition stores were hit.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center
damageLocations.description: Israeli F-35I jets destroyed centrifuge assembly halls and contaminated surrounding areas with low-level radioactive material at the Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan Power Generation Station
damageLocations.description: US Navy Tomahawk cruise missiles struck the power station supplying electricity to nuclear infrastructure, causing widespread blackouts across Isfahan province.

damageLocations.name: Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility
damageLocations.description: Multiple waves of Israeli aircraft delivered bunker busters on the deeply buried facility. IAEA confirmed underground centrifuge halls suffered catastrophic structural collapse.

damageLocations.name: Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant
damageLocations.description: Hardened facility buried inside a mountain near Qom was struck in sustained 45-minute assault. All access tunnels collapsed, facility rendered inoperable.

damageLocations.name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
damageLocations.description: Tomahawk missiles struck reactor cooling systems and auxiliary buildings. IAEA dispatched emergency teams for radiological contamination assessment.

damageLocations.name: IRIN Naval Base
damageLocations.description: Massive air and naval strike destroyed drydocks, missile boat pens, naval HQ building. Three frigates sunk at moorings. IRINS Dena subsequently sunk in Persian Gulf.

damageLocations.name: Shahid Doran Airbase
damageLocations.description: Israeli F-15I destroyed hardened aircraft shelters housing Iranian Su-35 fighters. 6 aircraft destroyed on ground along with radar installations.
damageLocations.casualties: 18 air force personnel killed, 6 aircraft destroyed

damageLocations.name: IRGC Regional HQ
damageLocations.description: Tomahawk cruise missiles destroyed main command building and barracks at IRGC Ground Forces regional headquarters.

damageLocations.name: Quds Force Training Facility
damageLocations.description: Social media reports of explosions near suspected IRGC Quds Force facility. Pentagon declined to comment. Satellite imagery showed burn scars consistent with cruise missile impacts.

damageLocations.name: 92nd Armored Division Base
damageLocations.description: Vehicle depots, fuel storage, and mobile missile launcher staging area destroyed. Secondary explosions continued for hours.

damageLocations.name: Minab Girls' Secondary School
damageLocations.description: US JASSM-ER struck building identified as suspected IRGC warehouse — actually a girls' school in session. Deadliest single incident of the war.

damageLocations.name: Parchin Military Research Complex
damageLocations.description: Underground chambers containing nuclear weapons research equipment destroyed. Massive explosions reported as explosive testing equipment detonated.

damageLocations.name: Centrifuge Component Factory
damageLocations.description: Multiple explosions near centrifuge component manufacturing facility. Satellite imagery showed significant damage to factory buildings.

damageLocations.name: IRGC Missile Storage Tunnels
damageLocations.description: Explosions reported near suspected underground missile storage tunnels. Iran condemned strikes near holy city religious sites.

damageLocations.name: IR-40 Heavy Water Reactor
damageLocations.description: Heavy water reactor struck by Israeli jets. Reactor vessel breached, cooling systems destroyed, facility permanently inoperable.

damageLocations.name: Underground Ballistic Missile Base
damageLocations.description: B-2 bombers struck hardened underground base housing Shahab-3 and Emad missiles. Massive secondary explosions from propellant detonation.

damageLocations.name: Kirya Military HQ Area
damageLocations.description: Iranian Emad and Shahab-3 missiles penetrated defenses, impacting near Kirya military HQ. Significant structural damage to surrounding buildings.

damageLocations.name: Ben Gurion International Airport
damageLocations.description: Missile debris and possible warhead impact near Ben Gurion Airport forced complete shutdown of Israel's main aviation hub. Runway damage reported but not officially confirmed.

damageLocations.name: Haifa Port & Oil Refinery
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket barrage struck port area and oil refineries. Direct hit on fuel storage tank triggered massive 3-day fire. Port operations halted indefinitely.

damageLocations.name: Jerusalem — Multiple Impact Sites
damageLocations.description: 9 successive missile waves over 4 hours overwhelmed defenses. Multiple impacts across city including near Old City walls. International condemnation swift.

damageLocations.name: Nevatim Air Base
damageLocations.description: Two warheads impacted base perimeter during Operation True Promise 3, damaging taxiways and a maintenance hangar. Home to Israel's F-35I fleet.

damageLocations.name: Negev Nuclear Research Center (Dimona)
damageLocations.description: Khorramshahr missiles reportedly directed at Dimona nuclear facility. Full media blackout imposed. Seismic sensors detected impacts but Israel stated all threats neutralized.

damageLocations.name: Beit Shemesh Residential Block
damageLocations.description: Iranian Emad warhead struck 4-story apartment block after evading Iron Dome. Building collapsed entirely. Rescue operations continued 36 hours.
damageLocations.source: IDF Home Front Command / Israeli Media

damageLocations.name: Ashdod Port
damageLocations.description: Iranian cruise missiles struck Israel's largest cargo port, hitting container yards and grain silo. Port operations suspended, shipping diverted to Eilat.

damageLocations.name: Ramat David Airbase
damageLocations.description: Combined drone/missile swarm with 50+ Shahed-136 drones saturating defenses. Fateh-110 missiles struck runway and flight line, destroying 3 F-16I aircraft.

damageLocations.name: Kiryat Shmona — City-wide Damage
damageLocations.description: 200+ Fajr-5 and Katyusha rockets caused widespread destruction to residential buildings, schools, and infrastructure. City mostly evacuated prior.

damageLocations.name: Al-Udeid Air Base
damageLocations.description: 3 missiles struck flight line, damaging KC-135 tankers and a C-17 transport at the largest US military facility in the Middle East.

damageLocations.name: Camp Arifjan
damageLocations.description: Fateh-110 missiles struck vehicle staging areas and warehouse complex, destroying dozens of MRAPs and supply containers.

damageLocations.name: Port Shuaiba Navy Logistics
damageLocations.description: Emad missile struck US Navy personnel quarters during early morning hours. Deadliest single US loss in opening hours of Iran's retaliation.

damageLocations.name: Al Dhafra Air Base
damageLocations.description: 2 cruise missiles struck taxiway infrastructure at base housing US F-35A fighters and MQ-9 Reapers. Flight operations temporarily halted.

damageLocations.name: Erbil Air Base
damageLocations.description: Precision missiles struck base perimeter, destroying communications equipment and drone hangar at US special operations facility.

damageLocations.name: Al-Asad Air Base
damageLocations.description: 15 Qiam-1 missiles targeted the base. 4 struck occupied facilities causing significant casualties despite partial evacuation to shelters.

damageLocations.name: NSA Bahrain (5th Fleet HQ)
damageLocations.description: Unconfirmed reports of strikes on US Fifth Fleet headquarters. Both Bahraini and US officials declined to comment. Residents reported air defense activations.

damageLocations.name: Ras Laffan LNG Terminal
damageLocations.description: World's largest LNG export terminal struck. Fires at two liquefaction trains, loading berth damaged. Global LNG prices surged 40% within hours.

damageLocations.name: Jebel Ali Port Complex
damageLocations.description: Unverified reports of explosions near the largest port in the Middle East. UAE denied incident but shipping satellite data showed vessels diverting for 18 hours.

damageLocations.name: Israeli Intelligence Listening Post
damageLocations.description: Iranian Mohajer-6 drones struck suspected Israeli intelligence post. Azerbaijan recalled ambassador. NATO member Turkey placed forces on alert.

damageLocations.name: Hezbollah Command Infrastructure (Dahieh)
damageLocations.description: Entire city blocks flattened in Beirut's southern suburbs targeting underground Hezbollah command bunkers. Lebanon called it disproportionate escalation.
damageLocations.casualties: 65+ killed including Hezbollah commanders, 200+ civilian casualties

damageLocations.name: Hezbollah Positions & UNIFIL Post
damageLocations.description: Israeli strikes hit Hezbollah rocket launchers and weapons storage. UN peacekeeping outpost damaged, UNIFIL evacuated.

damageLocations.name: Houthi Military Leadership Compound
damageLocations.description: US Tomahawk and MQ-9 strikes killed senior Houthi commander responsible for Red Sea anti-shipping attacks.
damageLocations.casualties: 20+ Houthi fighters killed including senior commander

damageLocations.name: Kharg Island Petroleum Infrastructure
damageLocations.description: Israeli F-35I strikes targeted petroleum export infrastructure on Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export terminal handling 90% of crude exports. Multiple storage tanks and loading facilities damaged.

damageLocations.name: Bat Yam Commercial District
damageLocations.description: Missile debris fell in commercial district. Military censors restricted reporting but social media showed shattered storefronts and car fires.

damageLocations.name: Sweida Building Strike
damageLocations.description: Iranian missile struck building in southern Syrian city. The attack highlighted the expanding geographic reach of Iranian retaliatory strikes.

damageLocations.name: RAF Akrotiri Runway
damageLocations.description: Iranian-made drone damaged runway at British RAF military base. First attack on European NATO territory in the conflict.

damageLocations.name: Al-Kharj Residential Area
damageLocations.description: Projectile struck residential facility south of Riyadh. First civilian deaths in Saudi Arabia during the conflict.

damageLocations.name: Salman Industrial City Desalination Plant
damageLocations.description: Hostile drone struck desalination plant causing fire. Attack on critical water infrastructure drew international condemnation.

damageLocations.name: Tehran Energy Infrastructure
damageLocations.description: IDF wide-scale strikes on Tehran energy resources and regime targets in new phase of conflict.

damageLocations.name: Doha (Intercepted Attack)
damageLocations.description: 17 ballistic missiles and 6 drones launched at Qatar — all intercepted by Qatari and allied air defense systems.

damageLocations.name: Sitra Residential Area
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missile struck residential apartment buildings in Sitra district. Significant structural damage to multiple buildings. 32 civilians wounded in the attack.

damageLocations.name: Manama Residential Building
damageLocations.description: Iranian missile struck a residential building in the Bahraini capital Manama, killing one civilian. The attack on a densely populated urban area drew international condemnation.

damageLocations.name: Eastern Tehran Residential Buildings
damageLocations.description: US-Israeli airstrike destroyed multiple residential buildings in eastern Tehran, killing at least 40 people. Oil facility strikes nearby caused toxic smoke to blanket the capital.

damageLocations.name: Arak Residential Building
damageLocations.description: Airstrike hit a residential building in Arak, western Iran. Five killed and several injured according to ISNA.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan UNESCO Heritage Sites
damageLocations.description: Strikes damaged multiple UNESCO World Heritage sites: Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Chehel Sotoun palace, Ali Qapu, Shah Mosque, and Teymouri Hall. Irreplaceable cultural heritage damaged.

damageLocations.name: Seef Commercial District
damageLocations.description: Building damaged in reported Iranian drone strike in the Seef commercial district of Manama, Bahrain.

damageLocations.name: Commercial Vessels — Strait of Hormuz (Day 12)
damageLocations.description: 3 more commercial ships attacked by IRGC Navy on Day 12, bringing total to 14 vessels damaged or sunk since war began. 330+ vessels at anchor. Shipping traffic down 95%. Insurance withdrawn by all major providers.

damageLocations.name: Khoramshahr Missile Impact Zone
damageLocations.description: Iran deployed Khoramshahr super-heavy ballistic missiles for the first time (37th wave). Iran's most powerful conventional missile with 1,800km range and 1,800kg warhead. Multiple intercepted by Arrow-3 system; damage assessment ongoing for those that penetrated defenses.

damageLocations.name: Moshav Haniel Residential Strike
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket directly struck and virtually destroyed a home in Moshav Haniel, central Israel. Building destroyed except reinforced safe room. Two residents survived.

damageLocations.name: Iraqi Oil Port Tanker Damage
damageLocations.description: Two foreign oil tankers attacked in Iraqi territorial waters, causing Iraq to shut down all oil port operations.

damageLocations.name: Beirut Corniche Seafront
damageLocations.description: Israeli overnight strikes hit Beirut's Corniche — a designated evacuation area. 8+ killed, buildings damaged along the seafront promenade.

damageLocations.name: Al-Nabi Shayth, Beqaa Valley
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike on Hezbollah positions in Beqaa Valley. 2 Hezbollah members killed, 6 injured.

damageLocations.name: Tehran (wide-scale wave)
damageLocations.description: New wave of Israeli strikes across Tehran. Part of 6,000+ total CENTCOM targets struck since Feb 28. Multiple explosions heard across the city.

damageLocations.name: KC-135 Crash Site near Turaibil
damageLocations.description: US KC-135 Stratotanker aerial refueling aircraft crashed near Turaibil, western Iraq. Circumstances under investigation.

damageLocations.name: Makhmour Coalition Base
damageLocations.description: Coalition base near Makhmour hit by drone strike attributed to Iran-backed Iraqi militias. One French soldier killed, several others wounded. France's first combat fatality in the conflict.

damageLocations.name: Oil Tankers in Iraqi Waters (Basra)
damageLocations.description: Multiple oil tankers struck in Iraqi waters near Basra by Iranian anti-ship missiles, part of expanding campaign against energy infrastructure in the Gulf.

damageLocations.name: Zarzir, Northern Israel
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missile impact in the northern town of Zarzir. Woman moderately wounded by shrapnel, 50+ injured by broken glass and debris.

damageLocations.name: Central Israel — Cluster Missile Impact Zone
damageLocations.description: 11 Iranian cluster missiles penetrated Israeli air defenses. One dropped approximately 70 submunitions over central Israel. 2 killed, multiple wounded.

damageLocations.name: Al-Quds Day Rally Site, Tehran
damageLocations.description: Explosion at Al-Quds Day rally in Tehran. 1 killed. Cause under investigation — rally was held in solidarity with Palestinians amid ongoing conflict.

damageLocations.name: Zrarieh Bridge (Litani River)
damageLocations.description: Israel destroyed the Zrarieh Bridge over the Litani River — first acknowledged strike on civilian infrastructure in Lebanon.

damageLocations.name: Tehran Al-Quds Day Rally Area
damageLocations.description: Explosion near Al-Quds Day rally in Tehran. 1 woman killed. Part of highest volume strikes day — 15,000+ total targets hit.

damageLocations.name: Beirut Targeted Strike Area
damageLocations.description: Israeli targeted killing attempt in Beirut kills several. Part of expanded campaign hitting areas never previously targeted. 25+ killed across Lebanon on Day 14.

damageLocations.name: Kharg Island Military Facilities
damageLocations.description: CENTCOM bombing raid destroys army defenses, Joshen Sea Base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar on Kharg Island. Oil infrastructure intact. 15+ explosions confirmed.

damageLocations.name: US Embassy Baghdad Helipad
damageLocations.description: Missile strikes helipad, drone damages radar system at US Embassy compound in Baghdad. Smoke seen rising from compound. Embassy structure intact.

damageLocations.name: Iran Space Research Center, Tehran
damageLocations.description: IDF destroys Iran's primary space research center and air defense factories in Tehran.

damageLocations.name: Fujairah Port Oil Loading Facilities
damageLocations.description: Drone attack causes fire, forces suspension of oil loading operations at Fujairah port — key Hormuz alternative.

damageLocations.name: KC-135 Crash Site, Western Iraq
damageLocations.description: KC-135 Stratotanker crash site. All 6 crew killed. CENTCOM says not hostile fire.

damageLocations.name: Kharg Island Military Facilities (90 targets)
damageLocations.description: CENTCOM struck 90 military targets — naval mine storage, missile bunkers, air defenses, naval base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared.

damageLocations.name: Ahmad Al-Jaber Air Base
damageLocations.description: Iranian drones hit Kuwait's Ahmad Al-Jaber Air Base as part of retaliatory strikes on Gulf state hosts of US forces.

damageLocations.name: Shiraz Military Installations
damageLocations.description: Multiple military installations near Shiraz struck — 20 explosions reported overnight

damageLocations.name: Dezful Air Base
damageLocations.description: Dezful Air Base damaged in overnight US-Israeli strikes

damageLocations.name: Residential Building, Northern Sidon
damageLocations.description: Residential building in northern Sidon struck by Israeli airstrike, killing 1 civilian and causing fire

damageLocations.name: Jask Port Facilities
damageLocations.description: Jask port naval facilities damaged by US cruise missile strike targeting Iranian naval infrastructure

damageLocations.name: Residential Area, Holon
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missile strikes residential area in Holon, Tel Aviv District. 2 killed, 12 injured. Multiple buildings damaged.

damageLocations.name: Residential Building, Bnei Brak
damageLocations.description: 4-story residential building upper floor struck by Iranian cluster munition. 3 wounded. Significant structural damage to upper floors.

damageLocations.name: Baghdad International Airport
damageLocations.description: Runway and airport facilities hit by 5 rockets. Flights suspended. 5 wounded. US Embassy issues security alert urging American citizens to leave Iraq.

damageLocations.name: Ali Al Salem Airbase Shelter
damageLocations.description: Italian Air Force MQ-9 Reaper drone destroyed in hangar by Iranian drone/rocket attack. First NATO ally hardware loss in the conflict. All personnel safe.

damageLocations.name: IRGC/Basij HQ, Hamedan
damageLocations.description: IRGC and Basij headquarters destroyed by IDF wide-scale strikes in Hamedan province, western Iran. Part of 200+ target wave operation.

damageLocations.name: Dubai International Airport Fuel Tank
damageLocations.description: Iranian drone struck fuel storage tank near Dubai International Airport causing massive fire. All flights suspended for 7+ hours. One of the world's busiest airports completely shut down.

damageLocations.name: Fujairah Oil Hub
damageLocations.description: Fire at Fujairah petroleum storage facility from Iranian drone/missile attack. Continued targeting of UAE energy infrastructure outside the Strait of Hormuz.

damageLocations.name: Khiam Town Infrastructure
damageLocations.description: IDF airstrikes on town infrastructure in Khiam supporting ground operation. Hezbollah positions and surrounding buildings damaged as Israeli troops enter the town.

damageLocations.name: Mehrabad Airport Military Section
damageLocations.description: IDF destroys Khamenei's personal aircraft and military hangars at Mehrabad Airport in Tehran. Satellite imagery confirms destruction of military section of the dual-use airport.

damageLocations.name: Al-Kharj Military Compound
damageLocations.description: 6 Iranian ballistic missiles struck military compound near Al-Kharj. 2 killed, 12 injured. Part of overnight barrage that included 60+ drones intercepted by Saudi air defenses.

damageLocations.name: Iran Space Research Center
damageLocations.description: Israel destroyed Iran's primary space research center used for anti-satellite weapons development and Chamran-1 satellite program.

damageLocations.name: Umm Al Quwain Building
damageLocations.description: Building targeted in drone attack, resulting in fire. No casualties.

damageLocations.name: Baghdad International Airport
damageLocations.description: 5 rockets struck airport area. Kataib Hezbollah claimed attack targeting Camp Victory. 5 wounded.

damageLocations.name: Rishon Lezion Residential Area
damageLocations.description: Cluster missile barrage damaged 10 locations (7 in Rishon Lezion, others in Lod and Shoham). One home largely destroyed.

damageLocations.name: Nahariya Residential Building
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket struck residential building causing fire. 6 wounded including 4 children.

damageLocations.name: Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (2nd strike)
damageLocations.description: Iranian drone hit oil zone for second time in 2 days. ADNOC suspended loading operations. UAE's only non-Hormuz oil export route.

damageLocations.name: Supreme Leader's Aircraft, Mehrabad Airport
damageLocations.description: IDF destroyed aircraft used by Iran's Supreme Leader at Mehrabad International Airport.

damageLocations.name: Shah Gas Field (South Pars Complex)
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike on Shah gas field at South Pars — world's largest natural gas field. Major fire reported. Shared with Qatar.

damageLocations.name: Al-Rasheed Hotel Area, Green Zone
damageLocations.description: US drone strike near Al-Rasheed Hotel in Green Zone killed IRGC commander al-Askari.
Cluster munitions struck 10+ locations in central Israel. Train station and residential buildings damaged. First confirmed use of cluster munitions in conflict.
Third consecutive day of strikes on Fujairah oil zone. Oil loading operations suspended. Commercial tanker hit.
US airstrikes hit residential areas in Arak, killing at least 14 civilians including children. Widespread destruction reported.
Israeli airstrikes on southern Beirut suburbs targeting Hezbollah command infrastructure during ground offensive.

damageLocations.name: Jerusalem Old City Holy Sites
damageLocations.description: Missile and interceptor fragments hit Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa Mosque, Church of Holy Sepulchre, Armenian Patriarchate, and Jewish Quarter.

damageLocations.name: Shah Gas Field
damageLocations.description: Iran struck UAE's Shah gas field, setting it ablaze. The field supplies 20% of UAE natural gas.

damageLocations.name: Nahariya Residential Area
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket hit residential area in Nahariya causing fire. 6 people wounded.

damageLocations.name: Karmiel Residential Home
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket hit a residential home in Karmiel. Residents survived in safe room.

damageLocations.name: Ramat Gan Residential (Stairwell)
damageLocations.description: IRGC missile strike killed elderly couple in residential building stairwell. Shrapnel also hit Tel Aviv Savidor Center train station.

damageLocations.name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant Premises
damageLocations.description: Projectile hit premises near metrological service building, close to operating power unit. IAEA confirmed no reactor damage.

damageLocations.name: US Embassy Baghdad (Outer Wall)
damageLocations.description: Explosive drone struck outer wall of US Embassy. Fire sparked. 2 other drones intercepted.

damageLocations.name: Beirut Kafaat/Haret Hreik
damageLocations.description: Israeli strikes on Kafaat and Haret Hreik areas. 6 killed, 24 injured.

damageLocations.name: South Pars Gas Field (Assaluyeh)
damageLocations.description: Gas storage tanks struck, two refineries halted (~100M cubic meters/day). World's largest gas field.

damageLocations.name: Beirut Bachoura Building
damageLocations.description: Building destroyed after evacuation order. 4th time struck. 6 killed.

damageLocations.name: Dorud Residential Area
damageLocations.description: Densely populated area struck. 7 killed, 56 injured.

damageLocations.name: Larestan Judiciary Facility
damageLocations.description: Judiciary facility struck, civilians and judicial staff killed/injured.

damageLocations.name: Ras Laffan LNG Facility
damageLocations.description: World's largest LNG export terminal (~20% global LNG). 'Extensive damage' from Iranian missile. Fires contained.

damageLocations.name: Hebron Beauty Salon
damageLocations.description: Iranian missile hit beauty salon near Hebron. 3 Palestinians killed.

damageLocations.name: Ras Laffan LNG (2nd Strike)
damageLocations.description: Second strike on world's largest LNG facility. Sizeable fires, extensive further damage.

damageLocations.name: South Pars Gas Treatment Facilities
damageLocations.description: 4 gas treatment plants at South Pars/Asaluyeh struck by IAF. Taken offline to contain fires. 36% of Iran's gas reserves affected.

damageLocations.name: Bandar Anzali Naval Base
damageLocations.description: IDF destroyed 4 missile boats, a corvette, and naval infrastructure at Bandar Anzali on Caspian Sea.

damageLocations.name: Ras Laffan LNG Complex
damageLocations.description: Iranian missile causes extensive damage to Ras Laffan Industrial City. Shell Pearl GTL plant also damaged. Fire extinguished, no casualties.

damageLocations.name: SAMREF Refinery, Yanbu
damageLocations.description: Iranian drone strikes SAMREF refinery in Red Sea port of Yanbu. Damage assessment underway.

damageLocations.name: Habshan Gas Facility
damageLocations.description: Abu Dhabi shuts Habshan gas facilities after debris from intercepted Iranian strike. Bab field also affected.

damageLocations.name: Ramat Gan Residential Area
damageLocations.description: Iranian revenge missile destroys apartment in Ramat Gan, killing couple in their 70s.

damageLocations.name: Bazan Oil Refinery Shrapnel Damage
damageLocations.description: Shrapnel from intercepted Iranian missile hit refinery, sparking fires. Extinguished quickly. 45-min power outage from damaged power line.

damageLocations.name: Kiryat Shmona Building Damage
damageLocations.description: Direct rocket hit on residential building. Fire damage. 4 civilians wounded.

damageLocations.name: Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery Fire
damageLocations.description: Iranian drone struck refinery unit. Fires extinguished. Combined capacity of targeted facilities: 730,000 bpd.

damageLocations.name: Bandar Anzali Naval Base — 5 Missile Boats Destroyed
damageLocations.description: First-ever strike on Iran's Caspian coast. 5 missile boats, naval HQ, and shipyard destroyed.

damageLocations.name: Ras Laffan LNG Facility — 17% Capacity Offline 3-5 Years
damageLocations.description: Second wave of Iranian ballistic missiles. Two LNG trains and one GTL facility damaged. $26B infrastructure damage. 12.8M tons/year LNG offline. QatarEnergy declares force majeure.

damageLocations.name: IRGC Command Center, Tehran/Karaj
damageLocations.description: IRGC command center struck — spokesman Gen. Naeini killed. Facility destroyed.

damageLocations.name: Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery (Day 2), Kuwait
damageLocations.description: 730K bpd refinery hit by Iranian drones for 2nd consecutive day. Multiple fires, units shut down.

damageLocations.name: SAMREF Refinery, Yanbu, Saudi Arabia
damageLocations.description: 400K bpd refinery (Aramco/ExxonMobil JV) struck by Iranian drone. Oil loading continued normally.

damageLocations.name: Warehouse, Bahrain
damageLocations.description: Company warehouse caught fire from shrapnel of intercepted Iranian projectile. Extinguished, no injuries.

damageLocations.name: Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery (Second Strike)
damageLocations.description: Second Iranian drone strike on Kuwait's primary oil refinery (730,000 bpd capacity). Fires broke out across refinery complex. Kuwaiti emergency response activated. Kuwait summoned Iran's ambassador. Refinery remains operationally impaired from prior strikes.

damageLocations.name: Tehran Eastern Districts — Nowruz Infrastructure Strikes
damageLocations.description: IDF launched strikes on Iranian regime infrastructure in eastern Tehran on Nowruz morning. IDF stated it targeted 'infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran.' IRGC spokesman Naini confirmed killed in strikes. Al Jazeera correspondent described the capital as hushed with no Nowruz celebrations visible.

damageLocations.name: Jerusalem Old City — Jewish Quarter (interception fragments)
damageLocations.description: Fragments from an intercepted Iranian ballistic missile landed in the Jewish Quarter of Jerusalem's Old City, approximately 400 meters from the Western Wall. No injuries reported. This represents the closest Iranian ordnance landing to Judaism's holiest accessible site.

damageLocations.name: Hezbollah Intelligence Headquarters — Beirut (Hussein Makled killed)
damageLocations.description: Israeli precision airstrike on Beirut killed Hussein Makled, identified as the head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters. Strike eliminated a key Hezbollah intelligence command node and represents ongoing IDF campaign to degrade Hezbollah leadership.

damageLocations.name: Bandar Anzali Naval Base
damageLocations.description: First Israeli strike on Iran's Caspian coast. IDF targeted Bandar Anzali naval base, destroying 5 Iranian Navy missile boats, damaging a headquarters building, and hitting a shipyard. First time Israel has struck the Caspian Sea region, opening a new geographic front in the war.

damageLocations.name: Mina Al-Ahmadi Oil Refinery (2nd strike)
damageLocations.description: Second consecutive day of Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery (730,000 bpd). Two drone waves sparked fires across multiple processing units. Mina Abdullah refinery also struck. One of the largest refineries in the Middle East with major global export significance.

damageLocations.name: Jerusalem Old City — Dung Gate wall/road
damageLocations.description: Fragment from intercepted Iranian ballistic missile struck road and wall near Dung Gate in Jerusalem's Jewish Quarter (~400m from Western Wall). Part of Day 21 (Nowruz) 8th Iranian ballistic missile barrage. Closest Iranian missile impact to holy sites in Jerusalem.

damageLocations.name: Rishon Lezion Daycare
damageLocations.description: Iranian cluster munition struck an empty daycare center in Rishon Lezion on Day 22. The building sustained significant structural damage from cluster submunitions. No casualties — the facility was empty at the time of the strike.

damageLocations.name: Natanz Nuclear Enrichment Facility
damageLocations.description: US-Israeli precision airstrikes struck Iran's primary centrifuge enrichment facility at Natanz on Day 22. Iranian state media Tasnim reported 'no radioactive leakage.' The facility housed Iran's main uranium enrichment centrifuges. Earlier strikes had targeted the site's power infrastructure; Day 22 strikes appear to target the main enrichment hall.

damageLocations.name: Jerusalem Old City — Jewish Quarter Road/Wall
damageLocations.description: Debris from an Iranian ballistic missile intercepted over Jerusalem landed in the Old City's Jewish Quarter, approximately 400 meters from the Western Wall and near the Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa Mosque. A road and wall section in the Jewish Quarter were damaged. No casualties.

damageLocations.name: Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility — 2nd Strike
damageLocations.description: Second major airstrike on the Natanz Shahid Ahmadi Roshan uranium enrichment complex. IAEA confirmed no radiation leak. Attack assessed as US-only bunker-buster operation. IDF denied involvement. First Natanz strike was on Day 1 (Feb 28). Repeated targeting further degrades Iran's enrichment capacity.

damageLocations.name: Mina al-Ahmadi Oil Refinery — Repeated Strikes
damageLocations.description: Kuwait's largest oil refinery (730,000 bpd capacity) struck by Iranian drones for the second consecutive day. Multiple unit fires. Kuwait national oil company shut down units. Part of Iran's systematic campaign targeting Gulf energy infrastructure.

damageLocations.name: Ballistic Missile Storage Complex — Western Iran
damageLocations.description: IDF struck a large-scale ballistic missile storage array with 5 near-simultaneous strikes in western Iran. Secondary explosions visible. Also struck: IRGC ballistic missile component production compound, missile production storage facility, and Ministry of Defense missile fuel production facility. IRGC missile personnel confirmed killed.

damageLocations.name: Arad Residential Buildings — 4 Buildings Damaged
damageLocations.description: Iranian IRBM direct impact in center of Arad between apartment buildings. Four buildings sustained extensive damage or partial structural collapse. Warhead estimated at 'hundreds of kilograms.' Air defense interceptors failed. IDF opened formal investigation into the interception failure.

damageLocations.name: Dimona Building — Adjacent to Negev Nuclear Research Center
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missile struck and destroyed a one-story building in Dimona. The Negev Nuclear Research Center (Israel's nuclear reactor complex) is located just outside the city — first strike on Israel's nuclear city. IAEA confirmed no damage to nuclear facility. Ghalibaf: 'The skies of Israel are unprotected.'

damageLocations.name: Arad Residential District (Direct Hit)
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missile direct hit on Arad residential neighborhood. Three buildings sustained massive damage. Air defense failure — IDF opened formal probe. 74 casualties (7 serious, 15 moderate, 52 light).

damageLocations.name: Dimona Residential Area (Near Negev Nuclear Research Center)
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missile struck residential area of Dimona, ~10 km from Negev Nuclear Research Center. Three-story building collapsed. 12 distinct impact sites. 47 hospitalized. IAEA: no nuclear facility damage, no radiation.

damageLocations.name: Rishon Lezion Daycare and Commercial Area (Cluster Munitions)
damageLocations.description: Iranian cluster munition warhead struck 11 sites in Rishon Lezion including an empty daycare/kindergarten. Debris from missile also fell near Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa in Jerusalem's Old City. Minor casualties.

damageLocations.name: Malek Ashtar University of Technology R&D Complex
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike targeted the R&D complex at Malek Ashtar University of Technology in East Tehran. The IDF stated the facility was used to develop nuclear weapon components and ballistic missile technology. The Malek Ashtar complex is Iran's premier defense engineering institution with known ties to IRGC weapons development programs.

damageLocations.name: Malek Ashtar University of Technology
damageLocations.description: Israel struck Malek Ashtar University of Technology's R&D complex in East Tehran on March 22 (Day 23). The IDF confirmed the facility was used to develop nuclear weapons components and ballistic missiles. The university is Iran's premier defense engineering institution, under international sanctions. Multiple explosions reported in eastern Tehran neighborhoods.

casualties.note: No casualty figures released by Iran.

damageLocations.name: Misgav Am residential area
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket struck Misgav Am residential area in northern Israel on March 22, killing civilian Ofer Moskovitz, 60. IDF investigation underway to determine if the projectile was Hezbollah or Israeli artillery fire.

damageLocations.name: Iranian Anti-Ship Missile Bunkers near Strait of Hormuz
damageLocations.description: US GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator 5,000-pound bunker buster bombs destroyed hardened underground Iranian anti-ship missile facilities overlooking the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM reported destruction of anti-ship cruise missile launchers, mobile missile systems, intelligence support sites, and ship-tracking radar relays. Part of CENTCOM's campaign that has now struck 8,000+ Iranian targets total.

damageLocations.name: Residential Buildings — Tel Aviv Cluster Munition Impact Sites
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missiles with cluster warheads scattered submunitions across 7+ sites in central Israel including Tel Aviv. Residential buildings and streets damaged. Cluster bomb fragments wounded 15 people across multiple neighborhoods. IDF confirmed cluster warhead type at multiple impact sites. Internationally condemned munition type used against civilian-populated area.

damageLocations.name: IRGC Missile Production Compound — Tehran
damageLocations.description: IDF overnight strikes destroyed 4 IRGC and Defense Ministry ballistic missile production and fuel facilities in Tehran. Israel said the strikes 'significantly damaged Iran's ability to produce key ballistic missile components.' Power substations in western Tehran and Karaj also struck, causing electricity outages.

damageLocations.name: Qasmiyeh Bridge — Southern Lebanon
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrikes destroyed the Qasmiyeh Bridge, a major coastal artery linking Sidon with southern Lebanon, as part of Defense Minister Katz's order to demolish all Litani River bridges. Lebanese President Aoun warned it was a 'prelude to ground invasion.'

damageLocations.name: Zrarieh Bridge — Southern Lebanon
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrikes destroyed the Zrarieh Bridge, the second Litani River crossing demolished in a single day as Israel executed Defense Minister Katz's order to destroy all Litani crossings. The bridges' demolition cuts off southern Lebanon logistically and signals preparation for expanded ground operations.

damageLocations.name: Nahariya Residential Area — Northern Israel
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket struck a residential area in the northern Israeli coastal city of Nahariya on Day 24, wounding six people. Sirens sounded across western Galilee. Ongoing cross-border rocket fire continues as IDF ground operations expand in southern Lebanon.

damageLocations.name: IRGC Police HQ / Basij Militia Base — Tehran
damageLocations.description: IDF launched a 'wide-scale wave' of strikes on Tehran targeting IRGC police headquarters, Basij militia command base, rocket engine production facility, and Isfahan Internal Security Force HQ. Al Jazeera's correspondent reported 'unprecedented' explosions on the eastern side of the city.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Unprecedented US-Israel strikes across Iran

damageLocations.name: Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant
damageLocations.description: CENTCOM struck and destroyed Iran's Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant, which manufactured gas turbine engines for IRGC attack drones and aircraft components. CENTCOM released before/after satellite imagery confirming the facility's total destruction.

sources.name: Business Standard — CENTCOM destroys Qom turbine plant

damageLocations.name: IRIB Radio Transmitter Station — Bandar Abbas
damageLocations.description: US-Israeli strike hit a 100-kilowatt AM transmitter of Iran's IRIB state broadcasting authority in Bandar Abbas, killing one IRIB security guard and injuring another. Bandar Abbas is home to Iran's primary naval base on the Strait of Hormuz.

damageLocations.name: Qasmiyeh Coastal Highway Bridge — Litani River
damageLocations.description: Israel struck the Qasmiyeh coastal highway bridge over the Litani River — the fifth Litani-area bridge struck since March 2. Defense Minister Katz had ordered all Litani River crossings demolished. Lebanon President Aoun called it a 'dangerous escalation' and 'prelude to a ground invasion north of the Litani.' Approximately 150,000 civilians in southern Lebanon face severely restricted movement.

sources.name: US News/AP — Israel orders destruction of bridges in Lebanon

damageLocations.name: Prince Sultan Airbase — IRGC Missile and Drone Strike
damageLocations.description: IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones at Prince Sultan Airbase — the primary US Air Force hub in the Arabian Peninsula housing F-35 and F-22 squadrons. Saudi Arabia confirmed two ballistic missiles were fired toward Riyadh; one was intercepted, one fell in uninhabited area south of capital. Extent of damage to the base not confirmed. IRGC simultaneously struck US 5th Fleet headquarters at Naval Support Activity Bahrain.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war Day 24 explainer
CBS News — Iran war live updates

damageLocations.name: Tabriz — Residential Buildings Strike
damageLocations.description: IDF/US strikes hit residential buildings in Tabriz, Iran's fourth-largest city. At least 6 civilians were killed and 6 injured. Tabriz crisis management official Farshi confirmed the deaths. Red Crescent rescue teams deployed. This represents one of the highest single-strike civilian death tolls in a major urban center outside Tehran during the conflict.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Unprecedented US-Israel strikes across Iran

damageLocations.name: Khorramabad — Residential Building Collapse
damageLocations.description: A US-Israeli airstrike hit residential buildings in Khorramabad (Lorestan Province). At least 1 child was killed — the body pulled from rubble by rescue teams. Multiple civilian casualties reported. Red Crescent deployed to the site. Khorramabad was simultaneously included in IDF warning for evacuation, suggesting more strikes may follow.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Unprecedented US-Israel strikes across Iran

damageLocations.name: IRGC Security Headquarters
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike destroyed the IRGC main security headquarters in Tehran as part of a 6-target overnight strike package. Also struck in the same package: IRGC Air Force weapons production site, Khatam-al Anbiya military emergency command center, Intelligence Ministry HQ, Defense Ministry weapons production facility, and army training base.

damageLocations.name: Iranian Intelligence Ministry Headquarters
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike struck the Iranian Intelligence Ministry headquarters in Tehran as part of overnight strike package targeting command and control infrastructure. The Intelligence Ministry oversees Iran's domestic and foreign intelligence services including operations against Israel.

damageLocations.name: Hezbollah HQ — Beirut Southern Suburbs (7 buildings)
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrikes hit 7 confirmed locations in Beirut's southern suburbs in one of the largest single-night strikes on the Lebanese capital. Lebanon's National News Agency confirmed 7 areas struck. Separate strikes hit gas stations across Lebanon. IDF says all Litani River bridges used by Hezbollah have been destroyed.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan Explosives Production Facility
damageLocations.description: Iran's main explosives production facility, struck by 120+ IDF bombs in overnight strike. Facility had been partially restored since June 2025 strike. IDF struck alongside dozens of other targets including missile launch sites.

damageLocations.name: IRGC Central Security Headquarters
damageLocations.description: IDF struck and destroyed the IRGC main central security headquarters in Tehran. Confirmed by IDF announcement and independent reporting. Part of a large-scale strike wave targeting 50+ targets including energy infrastructure.

damageLocations.name: Bushehr Nuclear Plant — Adjacent Structure
damageLocations.description: A structure approximately 350 metres from the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant was struck and destroyed. IAEA confirmed the proximity strike; reported no radiation release and no damage to the reactor itself. Second attack in the vicinity within one week.

damageLocations.name: PMF Operations HQ — Habbaniyah Base
damageLocations.description: US airstrike destroyed the PMF (Popular Mobilisation Forces) operations headquarters building at Habbaniyah base in Anbar province. 15 killed including Anbar commander Saad Dawai al-Baiji; 30 wounded. Deadliest single US strike on Iraqi militias since the conflict began.

damageLocations.name: Residential buildings — Bnei Brak / Petah Tikva cluster munition strike
damageLocations.description: Iranian cluster bomb munitions struck residential areas in Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva, and Rosh Ha'ayin. 12 injured including 6 children. Buildings and vehicles damaged by scattered bomblets.

damageLocations.name: Apartment blocks — Northern Tel Aviv ballistic missile impact
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missile struck northern Tel Aviv after interception failure. Large crater, multiple apartment blocks gutted. 4 injured. IAF investigating the defense failure.

damageLocations.name: Kuwait International Airport — Fuel depot drone strike (4th attack)
damageLocations.description: Iranian drones struck a fuel depot at Kuwait International Airport, triggering a fire. No casualties; material damage only. Fourth attack on the airport since Feb 28.

damageLocations.name: Hezbollah Al-Amana fuel stations + Litani River crossings — IDF strikes
damageLocations.description: IDF struck Hezbollah-linked Al-Amana fuel stations, Intelligence Directorate HQ in Beirut, Radwan Force command center, and all crossings along the Litani River used for weapons transfers. Terror tunnel near Mount Dov destroyed.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan Underwater Research Center — Iran's sole submarine R&D facility destroyed
damageLocations.description: IDF destroyed Iran's Underwater Research Center — the only site responsible for submarine planning and development for the Iranian navy. Two naval cruise missile production sites in central Tehran also struck with extensive damage.

damageLocations.name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — 2nd projectile strike, Rosatom evacuating
damageLocations.description: Projectile struck grounds of Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant at 9:08 PM local March 24 — second strike in two weeks. Iran reported no damage. Rosatom chief declared 'worst-case scenario', began 3rd evacuation phase. ~480 Russian staff remain.

damageLocations.name: Southern Tehran residential area — 12 killed, 28 wounded
damageLocations.description: Strike on residential area in southern Tehran killed 12 and wounded 28. School and residential buildings in eastern Tehran also destroyed.

damageLocations.name: Adloun + Mieh Mieh refugee camp — Israeli strikes kill 9 in southern Lebanon
damageLocations.description: Israeli strikes killed 9 in southern Lebanon: 4 in Adloun, 2 in Mieh Mieh Palestinian refugee camp (4 wounded), 3 elsewhere. Part of Israel's newly declared 'security zone' occupation to Litani River.

damageLocations.name: Qaaqaait al-Jisr Bridge — 5th and final Litani River crossing destroyed
damageLocations.description: IDF airstrike destroyed the Qaaqaait al-Jisr Bridge over the Litani River — the 5th and final Litani crossing to be blown up, completing Israel's isolation of south Lebanon. Defense Minister Katz confirmed all five Litani bridges have been destroyed. Approximately 150,000 civilians are trapped south of the Litani with no road connection northward.

damageLocations.name: Tel Aviv metro — IRGC Wave 80 debris and vehicle fire
damageLocations.description: IRGC Wave 80 fired four successive missile salvos at Tel Aviv metro area. Debris from one intercepted missile caused a vehicle fire in a central parking lot. 6 Israelis lightly injured at four sites across the metro area. Multiple interceptions over Tel Aviv, Hadera, and Bnei Brak. An Iranian ballistic missile was intercepted over Lebanese airspace for the first time.

damageLocations.name: Anbar Province PMF sites — US A-10 strikes, 7 killed
damageLocations.description: US A-10 Thunderbolt II aircraft struck Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) targets in Anbar Province, Iraq, killing 7 and wounding 10. Separately, pro-Iranian militia struck US positions including a radar position and a Black Hawk helicopter at Victoria Base near Baghdad, and the US consulate in Erbil. Iraq's National Security Council subsequently authorized PMF self-defense response.

damageLocations.name: Kuwait International Airport — Fuel Storage Tank
damageLocations.damage: Iranian drones struck a fuel storage tank at Kuwait International Airport, igniting a fire that was contained without casualties. Kuwait National Guard intercepted 6 additional drones. Kuwait Airways operations suspended temporarily.

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Fire at Kuwait airport after drones hit fuel tank

damageLocations.name: Tehran — Naval Cruise Missile Production Sites (2 facilities)
damageLocations.damage: IDF strikes in Tehran destroyed two naval cruise missile production sites as part of the revised targeting strategy focused on weapons manufacturing rather than regime-symbolic targets. Part of the same wave that struck the Isfahan Underwater Research Center.

sources.name: Haaretz — IDF shifts Iran strikes to military targets

damageLocations.name: Haifa Bay — Rocket Impacts (Wave 80 Hezbollah barrage)
damageLocations.damage: Hezbollah launched a barrage of 10 projectiles at the Haifa Bay area. IDF destroyed the rocket launcher shortly after launch. Some rockets were intercepted, others hit open areas with no injuries reported. IDF reservist seriously wounded in separate Hezbollah anti-tank attack near Lebanon border.

damageLocations.name: IRGC Navy Headquarters — Bandar Abbas (Tangsiri Killed)
damageLocations.description: Israeli precision strike on IRGC Navy HQ in Bandar Abbas killed Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri (architect of Hormuz blockade) and senior aides. The IRGC Navy command facility was struck in the early morning hours. Israel confirmed the strike via Defense Minister Katz.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan — Industrial and Military Infrastructure (Wave)
damageLocations.description: IDF completed a wave of strikes on Isfahan regime infrastructure, focusing on arms factories and military production facilities in advance of a possible ceasefire. The strikes are accelerating over the next 48 hours to degrade Iranian military capacity while diplomatic efforts continue. Isfahan is home to Iran's main ballistic missile development and production facilities.

damageLocations.name: Bnei Brak — Cluster Munition Impact (Wave 81)
damageLocations.description: Cluster munition debris from IRGC Wave 81 ('True Promise 4') struck Bnei Brak residential area, injuring 12 people. Part of the 81st IRGC wave that targeted 70+ Israeli sites with Emad, Qiam, Khorramshahr-4, and Qadr ballistic missiles.

damageLocations.name: Bandar Abbas — IRGC Naval HQ & 9th Air Force Airbase (Wave of Strikes)
damageLocations.description: Israeli strikes destroyed the IRGC naval headquarters in Bandar Abbas, killing IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri and two senior aides. CTP-ISW geolocated OSINT confirmed at least 4 strikes on the 9th Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase ammunition bunkers, causing large secondary explosions. A nearby residential area was also struck, killing two teenage brothers.
damageLocations.source: Euronews — IRGC Navy Chief Tangsiri killed in 'precise and lethal operation,' Israel says

damageLocations.name: Mashhad — 14th Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase
damageLocations.description: IDF struck the 14th Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase in Mashhad — the most northeastern target struck since the war began. First major strike on Iran's second-largest city. CTP-ISW OSINT geolocated via satellite imagery.
damageLocations.source: Alma Research Center — Daily Report Day 27

damageLocations.name: Tabriz International Airport — F-5 and F-4 jets destroyed
damageLocations.description: IDF struck Tabriz International Airport, destroying an F-5 and an F-4 fighter jet on the runway and damaging adjacent rail infrastructure. First Israeli strike on Tabriz, Iran's fourth-largest city. Located 100km from Turkish/Azerbaijani borders.
damageLocations.source: Al Jazeera — What's happening on Day 27 of US-Israel attacks on Iran

damageLocations.name: Nahariya — Hezbollah Rocket Strike on Parking Lot
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket struck a residential parking lot in Nahariya, killing one man and seriously wounding another. Three impact sites in the city. 14 physically wounded, 11 treated for acute anxiety. 10-rocket salvo fired from Lebanon.
damageLocations.source: Times of Israel — 1 dead, 14 wounded in Hezbollah rocket strike on Nahariya

damageLocations.name: IRGC Naval HQ, Bandar Abbas
damageLocations.description: IRGC Naval Headquarters struck in overnight airstrike that killed Navy Commander Tangsiri and two senior aides. Naval command and control infrastructure destroyed. IRGC Naval HQ was also the coordination center for the Hormuz blockade operations.

damageLocations.name: Kafr Qasim Residential Building
damageLocations.description: Residential building struck by Iranian cluster munition missile. Submunitions dispersed across wide area. 6 lightly wounded. Part of IRGC-Hezbollah coordinated Wave 82 attack.

damageLocations.name: Tabriz Airport (West Azerbaijan)
damageLocations.description: Tabriz International Airport struck by IAF — first strike in this northwestern city bordering Azerbaijan. Two F-5 and F-4 fighter jets destroyed on runway. Rail lines connecting Tabriz to western Iran also damaged.

damageLocations.name: Yazd Naval Missile and Sea Mine Production Facility
damageLocations.description: Iran's most central facility for developing, assembling, and storing advanced naval missiles and sea mines. Produced missiles for submarines, helicopters, and cruise platforms. Also manufactured sea mines deployed in the Strait of Hormuz blockade. IDF: significant blow to production capabilities of Iranian naval forces.

damageLocations.name: Tehran Weapon Manufacturing Complex (Multiple Sites)
damageLocations.description: Multiple weapon manufacturing facilities in Tehran and surrounding areas struck by IAF 60-jet wave (150+ munitions). Includes: weapon manufacturing facilities, anti-aircraft system storage base, ballistic missile component production site, battery production site, IRGC weapons production facility.

damageLocations.name: Kermanshah and Dezful Ballistic Missile Launch Sites
damageLocations.description: Ballistic missile launch and storage sites in Kermanshah and Dezful struck by ~20 IAF sorties dropping 70 munitions. Air defense systems also damaged. Sites used to launch ballistic missiles at Israel and Gulf states.

damageLocations.name: Shuwaikh Port
damageLocations.description: Main commercial port of Kuwait City struck by Iranian drones and cruise missiles at dawn on Day 28. Infrastructure damage to storage facilities confirmed by Kuwait Ports Authority. No casualties.

damageLocations.name: Ardakan Yellowcake Production Facility (Shahid Rezayee Nejad)
damageLocations.description: The only yellowcake uranium extraction facility in Iran, struck by Israel for the first time on Day 28. Critical step in nuclear fuel processing chain (uranium ore → yellowcake → enrichment). IAEA monitoring ongoing. No radiological risk reported.

damageLocations.name: Mobarakeh and Khuzestan Steel Plants
damageLocations.description: Two of Iran's largest steel production facilities struck by IDF on Day 28. 1 worker killed, 15 injured. IRGC issued evacuation warnings for US/Israeli-linked industrial workers across the Gulf in response.

damageLocations.name: Ardakan Yellowcake Production Plant
damageLocations.description: First-ever Israeli strike on the Ardakan Yellowcake Processing Plant in Yazd Province — the only yellowcake facility in Iran. Processes uranium ore from Saghand mine into yellowcake concentrate before enrichment. IAEA confirmed no nuclear material present, no radiological risk. Strategically significant: disrupts Iran's uranium processing supply chain.

damageLocations.name: Mobarakeh Steel Company (Isfahan)
damageLocations.description: IAF struck IRGC-linked Mobarakeh Steel Company, the largest steel producer in MENA. Power station and production line targeted. 1 worker killed, 2 wounded. Israel said the strike will "paralyze Iran's steel industry" with billions in damage. First targeting of a non-defense civilian industrial facility in the conflict.

damageLocations.name: Khuzestan Steel Company (Ahvaz)
damageLocations.description: IAF struck Khuzestan Steel Company near Ahvaz. Iran confirmed multiple steel plant strikes on March 27. Part of Israel's campaign to target IRGC-linked industrial infrastructure beyond traditional military and energy sites.

damageLocations.name: Shuwaikh Port (Kuwait City)
damageLocations.description: Iranian drones struck Shuwaikh Port — Kuwait City's main commercial port. Material damage reported, no casualties. Kuwaiti forces encountered 17 missiles and 13 drones total during the attack, intercepting 13 missiles and 10 drones.

damageLocations.name: Mubarak Al-Kabeer Port (Bubiyan Island, Kuwait)
damageLocations.description: Iranian drones and cruise missiles struck Mubarak Al-Kabeer Port on Bubiyan Island. Material damage, no casualties. This is a Chinese Belt and Road Initiative-linked port — reportedly the first time a Chinese-affiliated project in the Gulf was struck in the conflict. Part of the same IRGC attack package that hit Shuwaikh Port.

damageLocations.name: Kuwait International Airport — Radar System
damageLocations.description: Iranian drone attack significantly damaged the radar system at Kuwait International Airport. Kuwait civil aviation authority confirmed the attack, stating the radar was 'significantly damaged.' Civil aviation operations severely disrupted. Air defense intercepted 7 incoming projectiles.
damageLocations.source: Jerusalem Post — Kuwait International Airport attacked by several drones

damageLocations.name: Port of Salalah — Crane and Dock Infrastructure
damageLocations.description: Two Iranian drones struck the Port of Salalah, damaging a crane and causing limited infrastructure damage. This is the first strike on Omani territory in the conflict. Port operations were disrupted. One foreign worker was injured.
damageLocations.source: Oman Observer — Port of Salalah attacked by drones, expat worker injured

damageLocations.name: UAE Industrial Zone — Abu Dhabi Emirate
damageLocations.description: Iranian cruise missiles and drones struck a UAE industrial zone, triggering industrial fires and injuring 5 people. The UAE defense ministry confirmed the attack. UAE air defenses intercepted multiple incoming projectiles.

damageLocations.name: Al Manar/Al Mayadeen Press Location — Jezzine, South Lebanon
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike in the Jezzine area of south Lebanon struck a media position, killing Al Manar TV correspondent Ali Shoeib and Al Mayadeen reporter Fatima Ftouni. IDF claimed Shoeib was an IRGC intelligence operative using journalism as cover. Lebanese President Aoun condemned the strike as targeting press freedom.

damageLocations.name: Residential Building — Bourj Hammoud, Beirut
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike struck a residential apartment building in Bourj Hammoud, northeastern Beirut, killing at least one person and wounding four others. Smoke was visible across multiple neighborhoods in Beirut from the strikes.
damageLocations.source: CBS News — Live updates Iran war March 28

damageLocations.name: Salalah Container Port — Crane Infrastructure
damageLocations.description: Iranian drone strike destroyed a large container crane at Salalah Port, one of the Arabian Sea's largest container ports. Crane arms broken. Maersk suspended port operations for 48 hours. One foreign expatriate worker moderately injured.

damageLocations.name: Iran University of Science and Technology — Research Center
damageLocations.description: Israeli strike destroyed a research center building at the Iran University of Science and Technology. CNN geolocated videos showing the building demolished — twisted metal, bricks and debris. Nearby building on fire. University confirmed the strike, said no casualties, and condemned the attack as a violation of international law.

damageLocations.name: Iranian Naval Weapons and Ship Production HQ
damageLocations.description: IDF struck the headquarters of an Iranian naval weapons and ship producer in central Tehran during a widespread overnight strike wave. The facility was reportedly involved in production of anti-ship missiles and naval drones. Explosions heard across the city.

damageLocations.name: Ballistic Missile Production Facility — North Tehran
damageLocations.description: Israeli strikes targeted ballistic missile production facilities and storage sites in northern Tehran. Blasts in northern Tehran and smoke from the city's northeast reported by multiple witnesses. Part of IDF's 'widespread wave' on Tehran military infrastructure.
damageLocations.source: Al Jazeera — Iran war live Day 30

damageLocations.name: Tel Aviv University Campus — Cluster Munition Impact Sites
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missiles carrying cluster munitions struck 8 sites across Tel Aviv, including at a university campus. CNN journalists on the ground witnessed cluster munitions exploding over the city. 1 civilian killed, 2 injured. Cluster munition submunitions scattered across multiple neighborhoods.

damageLocations.name: Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) — Smelter Complex
damageLocations.description: IRGC struck the Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) smelter complex in a combined missile and drone attack. Alba is one of the world's largest single-site aluminium smelters. IRGC claimed it is 'affiliated with US military and aerospace sectors.' Two employees lightly wounded. Damage to facility infrastructure confirmed by Al Jazeera.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — IRGC claims attacks on UAE, Bahrain aluminium facilities
CNBC — Bahrain aluminium giant says Iranian attack targeted its facility

damageLocations.name: Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) — Al-Taweelah, Khalifa Economic Zone
damageLocations.description: IRGC struck Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) at the Al-Taweelah site within Abu Dhabi's Khalifa Economic Zone. EGA is one of the world's largest aluminium producers. 'Significant damage' to facility per Al Jazeera. Multiple employees injured (non-life-threatening).

sources.name: Al Jazeera — IRGC claims attacks on UAE, Bahrain aluminium facilities
New Arab — Iran Guards say struck Bahrain, UAE aluminium plants

damageLocations.name: E-3G AWACS + KC-135 aircraft — Prince Sultan Air Base
damageLocations.description: Open-source imagery confirmed total destruction of US Air Force E-3G Sentry AWACS (tail 81-0005) at Prince Sultan Air Base in the March 27 IRGC attack. Fuselage split in two; rotodome displaced. One KC-135R Stratotanker also completely destroyed; three others severely damaged. First US strategic aircraft destroyed in combat since the Cold War. 15+ US troops wounded.

sources.name: Air & Space Forces Magazine — Key E-3 AWACS Damaged/Destroyed
The War Zone — Images Show E-3 Sentry Totally Destroyed

damageLocations.name: ADAMA Makhteshim Agricultural Chemical Plant
damageLocations.city: Ne'ot Hovav Industrial Zone, Southern Israel
damageLocations.description: An Iranian ballistic missile fragment struck the ADAMA (Syngenta Group) Makhteshim agricultural chemical plant in the Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone. A fire started at the facility, triggering fears of hazardous chemical release. Home Front Command issued shelter-in-place orders for the Ne'ot Hovav zone; Highway 40 closed; three hazmat teams deployed. Environmental Protection Minister Silman later issued an all-clear after monitoring showed no chemical dispersal. The plant manufactures organophosphate-based pesticides, herbicides, and fungicides.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — Iranian missile hit on Ne'ot Hovav factory leads to fear of chemical leakage
Times of Israel — ADAMA says its plant in southern Israel was hit

damageLocations.name: Khondab Heavy Water Research Reactor (IR-40)
damageLocations.description: IAEA confirmed on March 29 that Iran's Khondab heavy water research reactor (IR-40) near Arak 'sustained severe damage and is no longer operational.' The IAEA noted no declared nuclear material was present at the time. The IR-40 was a 40MW heavy water reactor that had been redesigned under the 2015 JCPOA agreement and was a key element of Iran's civilian nuclear program. This marks the second major confirmed nuclear facility rendered inoperable during the conflict, after Fordow.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF confirms bombing Iran's heavy water reactor at Arak
Türkiye Today — US-Israeli strikes hit Iran's Khondab facility at Arak

damageLocations.name: Tabriz Petrochemical Company
damageLocations.description: IDF airstrike ignited a major fire at the Tabriz Petrochemical Company — one of Iran's largest petrochemical facilities. Tabriz is Iran's fourth-largest city. The strike marks a significant IDF escalation targeting civilian industrial infrastructure beyond strictly military targets. Several workers reported injured. Fire visible from city center.

damageLocations.name: Bazan Oil Refinery (Haifa)
damageLocations.description: Iranian barrage (ballistic missiles + Hezbollah rockets) struck the Bazan Oil Refinery in Haifa — Israel's largest oil refinery, processing ~9.8 million tonnes/year (~25% of Israel's total refining capacity). Major fire broke out at a processing unit. One person lightly wounded. First confirmed hit on a major Israeli energy processing facility in the conflict.

damageLocations.name: Kuwait Power and Desalination Plant
damageLocations.description: Iranian drone struck a power and desalination plant in Kuwait, killing one Indian national worker and injuring several others. Kuwait filed emergency diplomatic protests. Marks the first confirmed third-country civilian death from Iranian strike on Gulf civilian infrastructure. Damage to desalination capacity threatens water supply in a water-scarce country.

damageLocations.name: Martyr Mohammed Alaa Air Base — Aircraft Destroyed
damageLocations.description: Iraqi militia rockets struck the Martyr Mohammed Alaa Air Base in Baghdad, destroying one aircraft. No injuries reported. This is part of the ongoing Islamic Resistance in Iraq (IRI) militia campaign against US-associated military facilities. The US Embassy Baghdad issued a security alert on March 29 urging all US citizens to leave Iraq.

IRGC drone struck the Kuwaiti VLCC 'Al-Salmi' at anchor in Dubai port area just after midnight March 31. Fire broke out on hull-damaged vessel. All 24 crew evacuated. Oil spill risk. First Iranian strike on a commercial vessel in port.

sources.name: Bloomberg — Iran Strikes Kuwait Oil Tanker in Dubai Port

damageLocations.description: Debris from intercepted Iranian ballistic missile caused fires in Abu Dhabi, injuring 6 people.

sources.name: Alma Research Center — Daily Report March 30

damageLocations.description: Iranian drones struck a Kuwaiti military camp, injuring 10 servicemen. Part of broader attack wave (14 ballistic missiles + 12 drones).

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iranian attack on Kuwait

damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike destroyed permanent LAF checkpoint at al-Amiriyah. 1 soldier killed, 5 wounded. First direct Israeli strike on permanent LAF installation.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Lebanese soldier killed

damageLocations.description: Missile struck gasoline storage tank at Bazan Group refinery. Fire with thick black smoke. 2 injured (1 serious). 2nd hit during war (first March 19). Fire contained; Energy Minister said no production damage.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Fire after missile attack on Haifa refinery

damageLocations.description: Strike on orphanage in Alborz province west of Tehran. 2 killed, 5 injured per Fars News Agency.

sources.name: CGTN — Iran says US, Israel strike orphanage

damageLocations.city: Bnei Brak / Ramat Gan / Petah Tikva
damageLocations.description: Iranian cluster munition warhead dispersed ~24 bomblets over central Israel. Multiple cars set ablaze, workshop fire in Petah Tikva. 12 wounded including 6 children.
IRGC drone struck Kuwaiti VLCC Al-Salmi at Dubai anchorage. Fire contained. No spill. 24 crew safe.
Overnight US-Israeli strike hit Tabriz petrochemical unit. Fire contained per Fars.
Israeli airstrike hit LAF checkpoint. First strike on permanent LAF installation. 1 soldier killed, 5 wounded.
Intercepted Iranian drone debris fell on 6 houses. No casualties. Part of 10 drone + 8 missile overnight wave.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan Ammunition Arsenals
damageLocations.description: US bunker-buster strikes destroyed ammunition arsenals, underground missile city, weapons depots.

damageLocations.name: Petah Tikva Workshop
damageLocations.description: Iranian missile struck workshop area, causing fires. Cars destroyed.

damageLocations.name: Zanjan Grand Husseiniya
damageLocations.description: Shia religious center damaged in US/Israeli airstrike.

damageLocations.name: Tofiq Daru Chemical Factory
damageLocations.description: Pharmaceutical/chemical factory destroyed by IDF. Accused of supplying fentanyl for chemical weapons R&D.

damageLocations.name: Bnei Brak Residential House
damageLocations.description: Direct hit from Iranian cluster munition submunition. Mother and two sons lightly injured.

damageLocations.name: Khalde Highway Vehicles
damageLocations.description: IDF airstrike destroyed vehicles on Khalde highway targeting Hezbollah commander. 7 killed, 24 wounded total across 2 Beirut strikes.

damageLocations.name: Mobarakeh Steel Complex (2nd strike)
damageLocations.description: Iran's largest steel plant struck for second time by US-Israeli forces. Massive fireballs. Fars news confirmed.

damageLocations.name: Shahid Haqqani Port
damageLocations.description: Port facility in Bandar Abbas struck by IDF as part of Tehran infrastructure wave.

damageLocations.name: Kuwait Airport KAFCO Fuel Tanks
damageLocations.description: Iranian drone struck KAFCO fuel tanks at Kuwait International Airport, igniting massive fire. No casualties.

damageLocations.name: QatarEnergy Tanker Aqua 1
damageLocations.description: Iranian cruise missile hit fuel oil tanker 17nm north of Ras Laffan. Hull damage above waterline. 21 crew evacuated.

damageLocations.name: Grand Hosseinieh of Zanjan Mosque Complex
damageLocations.description: Mosque complex admin/cultural buildings destroyed in US-Israeli airstrike. 4 killed, 26 wounded.

damageLocations.name: Qeshm Island Desalination Plant
damageLocations.description: Desalination plant on Qeshm Island knocked out of service by strikes. Affects civilian water supply in Strait of Hormuz area.

damageLocations.name: Former US Embassy Area — Tehran
damageLocations.description: Strikes hit area near former US embassy in Tehran, damaging some of the building's walls.

damageLocations.name: Fentanyl Factory (Chemical Weapons-linked)
damageLocations.description: IDF struck factory it claims produced fentanyl for Iran's chemical weapons program.

damageLocations.name: Kharazi residence / diplomatic target — Tehran
damageLocations.description: Israeli airstrike struck location where former FM Kamal Kharazi was present. Kharazi seriously injured, wife killed. Kharazi was overseeing diplomatic back-channel with Pakistan for possible Vance-Iran meeting. Strike seen as targeting Iran's diplomatic apparatus.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan Military Installation (bunker-buster)
damageLocations.description: Massive bunker-buster strikes caused largest secondary explosions of the war. Isfahan steel plant struck for second time.

damageLocations.name: Tofigh Daru Pharmaceutical Company, Tehran
damageLocations.description: Cancer drug and pharmaceutical facility struck. Drug production line damaged.

damageLocations.name: Hengam Island Defenses, Strait of Hormuz
damageLocations.description: Airstrikes on strategic island in Hormuz arch defense chain. 7 injured.

damageLocations.name: Shahid Haqqani Passenger Pier, Bandar Abbas
damageLocations.description: Civilian passenger pier bombed. Qeshm Island desalination plant knocked out of service.

damageLocations.name: KAFCO Fuel Tanks, Kuwait International Airport
damageLocations.description: Iranian drones struck fuel storage tanks operated by KAFCO. Massive fire. Airport remains closed to civilian operations.

damageLocations.name: Shia Religious Site, Zanjan
damageLocations.description: Strike hit Shia religious site in northwestern Iran.

damageLocations.name: Residential Buildings, Bnei Brak (Passover Eve)
damageLocations.description: Iranian cluster missile struck residential area on Passover eve. 14 wounded including 11-year-old girl critical. Extensive damage to homes and vehicles in Bnei Brak, Rosh HaAyin, and Petah Tikva.

damageLocations.name: Sewage Infrastructure, Bnei Brak (Overnight April 2)
damageLocations.description: Iranian cluster bomblet damaged sewage pipes in Bnei Brak causing flooding. 4 wounded overnight including 2 babies. Impact sites also in Tel Aviv city center.

damageLocations.name: Kharazi Residence, Ajouraniyeh, Tehran
damageLocations.description: Airstrike hit home of former Iranian FM Kamal Kharazi in Tehran's Ajouraniyeh neighborhood. Kharazi seriously wounded, wife killed. He was overseeing diplomatic back-channel with Pakistan for Vance-Iran meeting.

damageLocations.name: Building in Shfaram, Northern Israel
damageLocations.description: Hezbollah rocket from Lebanon hit building in Druze city of Shfaram east of Haifa. Part of 50+ rocket barrage during Passover.

damageLocations.name: Pasteur Institute of Iran, Tehran
damageLocations.description: US-Israeli airstrike severely damaged the Pasteur Institute of Iran, a century-old medical research center (founded 1920) that serves as Iran's leading center for vaccine development and infectious disease research. Iran Health Ministry: 'barbaric assault on international health security.'

damageLocations.name: Tallest Bridge Under Construction, Western Tehran
damageLocations.description: US-Israeli strikes destroyed a major bridge under construction in western Tehran — set to become the tallest bridge in the Middle East. Near completion. Several people injured.

damageLocations.name: Mobarakeh Steel Complex (Full Shutdown), Isfahan
damageLocations.description: Iran's largest steel producer completely shut down after repeated US-Israeli strikes. All steelmaking furnaces damaged. Khuzestan Steel in Ahvaz also struck. Recovery estimated 6-12 months. Iran calls it 'economic terrorism.'

damageLocations.name: Industrial Buildings, Petah Tikva
damageLocations.description: Iranian ballistic missile with cluster warhead struck industrial buildings between residential and industrial zones. Bomblets spread to a nearby playground. Homes and vehicles damaged. 5 wounded including 11-year-old girl in critical condition.

damageLocations.name: B1 Bridge, Karaj Northern Bypass
damageLocations.description: Iran's tallest bridge (B1 Bridge on Karaj Northern Bypass) completely collapsed after two waves of US airstrikes. Civilians celebrating Nowruz Nature Day gathered under the bridge. 8 killed, 95 wounded. Trump posted: 'much more to follow.' Bridge connected Tehran to western regions.

damageLocations.name: Amazon AWS Cloud Facility, Bahrain
damageLocations.description: IRGC missile strike damaged Amazon Web Services cloud computing facility — fourth attack on Amazon Bahrain operations since war began. Part of IRGC campaign against 18 named US tech companies.

damageLocations.name: Pasteur Institute, Tehran
damageLocations.description: US/Israeli strikes heavily damaged Iran's Pasteur Institute in Tehran — century-old medical research center founded in 1920 to combat infectious diseases including plague and smallpox.

damageLocations.name: Isfahan Steel Plants
damageLocations.description: Israeli strikes shut down Iran's two largest steel production facilities. Restart estimated 6-12 months, threatening to cripple one of Iran's most important industrial sectors.

damageLocations.name: Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery, Kuwait
damageLocations.description: Iranian drones struck Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery (~466K bpd) for third time since war began. Fires in operational units, contained by emergency teams. No injuries.

damageLocations.name: Kuwait Desalination Plant
damageLocations.description: Missile struck Kuwaiti water desalination plant. Kuwait blamed Iran; IRGC denied, blamed Israel. Water infrastructure attack represents dangerous escalation of civilian targeting.

damageLocations.name: B1 Bridge (Tehran-Karaj Highway)
damageLocations.description: B1 Bridge — tallest bridge in Middle East connecting Tehran to Karaj — completely collapsed after two-wave US precision strikes. 8 killed, 95 wounded. Major drone/missile supply route severed.

damageLocations.name: Red Crescent Aid Warehouse
damageLocations.description: Drone strike destroyed Red Crescent aid warehouse — two containers, two buses, emergency vehicles destroyed. Third IFRC personnel death since war began.

damageLocations.name: Habshan Gas Facility (ADNOC)
damageLocations.description: Interceptor debris from Iranian missiles caused fire at ADNOC's main gas processing complex. Operations halted. 12 injured in Ajban area from falling debris.

damageLocations.name: Batelco HQ / AWS Data Centers
damageLocations.description: Iranian strikes hit Batelco telecom HQ hosting Amazon Web Services data centers. Power outages disrupted banking and airline systems. First strike on US tech company infrastructure.

damageLocations.name: Petah Tikva (near school)
damageLocations.description: Unintercepted Iranian missile cratered near school. Cluster bomblets damaged homes, roads, vehicles in Petah Tikva, Ramat Gan, and Givat Shmuel. 3 lightly wounded.

damageLocations.name: Sohmor and Mashghara Bridges
damageLocations.description: IDF destroyed bridges over Litani River. Total destroyed Litani crossings reaches 8, effectively isolating western Bekaa from rest of Lebanon.
